intent: Package SecurityRecipes as an enterprise assurance layer for agentic remediation: every workflow, MCP gateway decision, prompt supply-chain surface, reviewer gate, and evidence stream can be exported for buyers, auditors, AI platform teams, and security leadership.

positioning.buyer: Security Engineering, AI Platform, Product Security, GRC, and engineering executives
positioning.enterprise_promise: A machine-readable trust pack that makes agentic remediation reviewable, enforceable, and measurable before it is scaled across an enterprise.
positioning.why_now: Agentic AI security guidance has moved from general LLM risk to tool-using, autonomous, MCP-connected systems. Enterprise buyers need evidence that agents are governed at runtime, not just prompted carefully.

standards_alignment.name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework 1.0
standards_alignment.coverage: Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage functions for AI risk programs.

standards_alignment.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
standards_alignment.coverage: Generative AI-specific governance, data, measurement, and lifecycle risks.

standards_alignment.name: NIST SP 800-218 Secure Software Development Framework
standards_alignment.coverage: Secure development and vulnerability-response evidence for agent-authored changes.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications
standards_alignment.coverage: Prompt injection, sensitive information disclosure, supply chain vulnerabilities, insecure plugin design, excessive agency, and overreliance.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
standards_alignment.coverage: Agent goal hijack, tool misuse, identity and privilege abuse, agentic supply chain, unexpected code execution, memory and context poisoning, cascading failures, and rogue agents.

standards_alignment.name: Cloud Security Alliance AI Controls Matrix
standards_alignment.coverage: Vendor-neutral control objectives for secure and responsible cloud-based AI systems.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Security Best Practices
standards_alignment.coverage: Confused deputy prevention, token-passthrough avoidance, scope minimization, session safety, and MCP-specific auditability.

standards_alignment.name: CISA Secure by Design
standards_alignment.coverage: Executive ownership, measurable security outcomes, transparency, and safe defaults for software and AI systems.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP AI Bill of Materials Project and Generator
standards_alignment.coverage: AI system transparency through inventories of models, data, dependencies, provenance, and risk-relevant components.

evidence_artifacts.title: Generated MCP gateway policy pack
evidence_artifacts.retention: per repository history and release artifact retention

evidence_artifacts.title: Workflow control-plane validation report
evidence_artifacts.retention: per repository history and release artifact retention

evidence_artifacts.title: Generated agentic red-team drill pack
evidence_artifacts.retention: per repository history and release artifact retention

evidence_artifacts.title: Prompt, skill, and rules-file source tree
Runtime MCP gateway audit log
evidence_artifacts.produced_by: enterprise MCP gateway or agent host

controls.title: Workflow Inventory and Ownership
controls.objective: Every agentic remediation workflow has a declared owner, status, maturity stage, eligible finding classes, public documentation path, and escalation path.
controls.ai_easy_default: Agents and reviewers can ask for one workflow ID and receive the approved scope, owner, gates, and evidence contract without reading multiple docs pages.
controls.buyer_value: Turns a collection of recipes into a governed deployment inventory that GRC and AI platform teams can approve.

framework_mappings.control: Govern and Map
framework_mappings.rationale: The workflow manifest establishes ownership, context, and intended use before an AI system acts.

framework_mappings.control: Governance, Risk Management, and AI Asset Management
framework_mappings.rationale: The manifest is the AI remediation asset inventory and control owner register.

framework_mappings.control: Executive ownership and transparency
framework_mappings.rationale: Workflows name accountable teams and measurable operating expectations.

controls.title: Default-Deny Tool Access
controls.objective: Agent tool calls are denied by default and allowed only inside declared MCP namespaces, access modes, branch prefixes, and changed-file limits.
controls.ai_easy_default: The policy pack gives an enforcer a simple allow, hold, deny, or kill decision instead of expecting the model to remember policy text.
controls.buyer_value: Makes agent access enforceable at the platform layer and limits tool misuse before it becomes an incident.

framework_mappings.control: Scope minimization, confused deputy prevention, and token-passthrough avoidance
framework_mappings.rationale: The gateway policy narrows each run to explicit namespaces and access modes.

framework_mappings.control: ASI02 Tool Misuse and ASI03 Identity and Privilege Abuse
framework_mappings.rationale: Default-deny tool scopes reduce misuse of legitimate tools and limit privilege inheritance.

framework_mappings.control: Insecure plugin design and excessive agency
framework_mappings.rationale: Scoped, auditable tool access prevents broad plugin authority from being delegated to the model.

controls.title: Human Review and Separation of Duties
controls.objective: Agents can propose remediations but cannot merge them; source-host controls and reviewer pools preserve human accountability for change approval.
controls.ai_easy_default: The workflow contract tells the agent exactly when to stop and what review evidence to attach instead of negotiating approvals in chat.
controls.buyer_value: Lets enterprises scale AI-authored fixes while preserving the change-management controls their auditors already expect.

framework_mappings.control: PW.7 review and analyze code; RV.1 identify and confirm vulnerabilities
framework_mappings.rationale: Agent-authored changes remain subject to review, scanner confirmation, and finding-to-PR traceability.

framework_mappings.control: Measure and Manage
framework_mappings.rationale: Reviewer gates measure output quality and manage residual risk before deployment.

framework_mappings.control: Secure outcomes over user burden
framework_mappings.rationale: The platform owns safe defaults while humans approve risk-bearing changes.

controls.title: Prompt, Skill, and MCP Supply Chain Governance
controls.objective: Prompts, skills, rules files, workflow manifests, and MCP client/server configuration are treated as reviewed software supply-chain artifacts.
controls.ai_easy_default: Agents consume pinned, reviewed instructions and policy artifacts instead of ad hoc prompt fragments.
controls.buyer_value: Protects the highest-leverage control surface in agentic systems: the instructions and tools that shape every run.

framework_mappings.control: ASI04 Agentic Supply Chain Vulnerabilities and ASI06 Memory and Context Poisoning
framework_mappings.rationale: Reviewed, versioned prompt and MCP artifacts reduce poisoning and instruction drift.

framework_mappings.control: Supply chain vulnerabilities and prompt injection
framework_mappings.rationale: Instruction files and context sources are governed as code rather than unmanaged content.

framework_mappings.control: AI system component transparency
framework_mappings.rationale: Prompt, tool, model, and MCP dependencies are inventory candidates for an AI Bill of Materials.

controls.title: Evidence Chain of Custody
controls.objective: Each agentic run produces finding linkage, tool audit, scanner before/after evidence, reviewer decisions, KPI records, and retention ownership.
controls.ai_easy_default: The evidence contract is generated from the manifest, so every workflow tells agents which records to attach before the PR is reviewable.
controls.buyer_value: Shortens security reviews, procurement reviews, and audits by turning operational evidence into a portable export.

framework_mappings.control: Measure and Manage
framework_mappings.rationale: Evidence streams support ongoing risk measurement and control adjustment.

framework_mappings.control: Auditability, logging, monitoring, and assurance
framework_mappings.rationale: Evidence records assign sources, retention, and owners to every workflow.

framework_mappings.control: Measurable security outcomes
framework_mappings.rationale: The pack reports workflow coverage, gates, KPIs, and failure counts.

controls.title: Runtime Kill Signals and Session Disablement
controls.objective: Agent sessions stop immediately on declared runtime risk signals such as scope creep, credential access attempts, release attempts, repeated failed loops, or missing verifier coverage.
controls.ai_easy_default: Agents get clear stop conditions and enforcers get a kill_session decision instead of relying on model self-restraint.
controls.buyer_value: Creates a credible blast-radius limit for autonomous work across repositories, MCP servers, registries, and payment or protocol contexts.

framework_mappings.control: ASI08 Cascading Failures and ASI10 Rogue Agents
framework_mappings.rationale: Runtime stop conditions prevent small failures from propagating and cut off rogue behavior.

framework_mappings.control: Session safety and local MCP server compromise mitigations
framework_mappings.rationale: Session disablement and scoped egress reduce harm when tool context becomes unsafe.

framework_mappings.control: Manage generative AI risks across deployment and operation
framework_mappings.rationale: Kill signals are operational risk controls for deployed agentic workflows.

controls.title: Model and Data Handling Boundaries
controls.objective: Each workflow records what data classes, model-provider constraints, regions, retention settings, and external systems are allowed before the agent runs.
controls.ai_easy_default: The workflow's MCP context and evidence contract make data exposure explicit rather than hidden in agent prompts.
controls.buyer_value: Gives AI Platform and Legal a durable review surface for model-provider, data-residency, and retention risk.

framework_mappings.control: Data governance, third-party dependency, and lifecycle risk management
framework_mappings.rationale: Workflow manifests and provider contracts record what data can reach models and connected services.

framework_mappings.control: Sensitive information disclosure
framework_mappings.rationale: Data classes and scoped context reduce unintended exposure through prompts and tool calls.

framework_mappings.control: Data security, privacy, and third-party AI service governance
framework_mappings.rationale: Provider boundaries and runtime logs support cloud AI control assurance.

controls.title: AI and Agent Bill of Materials Readiness
controls.objective: SecurityRecipes exports the components needed to build an AI/Agent BOM: workflows, models or agent classes, MCP namespaces, prompt assets, policy packs, evidence owners, and runtime dependencies.
controls.ai_easy_default: Downstream platforms can ingest a single assurance pack to inventory agentic remediation capabilities and their control dependencies.
controls.buyer_value: Positions the site for AI transparency, procurement, and vendor-risk workflows that are becoming standard enterprise asks.

framework_mappings.control: AI Bill of Materials transparency
framework_mappings.rationale: The pack inventories agentic components, policy dependencies, and evidence owners.

framework_mappings.control: Transparency and secure defaults
framework_mappings.rationale: A BOM-ready export helps customers evaluate AI system security before deployment.

framework_mappings.control: Agentic supply chain and identity-aware operations
framework_mappings.rationale: Inventorying agent, prompt, tool, and MCP dependencies is the first step toward agentic supply-chain control.

controls.title: Adversarial Evaluation and Red-Team Replay
controls.objective: Approved agentic remediation workflows have repeatable adversarial drills for tool-result injection, goal hijack, credential access, approval bypass, token passthrough, connector drift, runaway loops, and evidence integrity failures.
controls.ai_easy_default: Agents, gateways, and reviewers can load one generated drill pack to know which hostile inputs to replay and which evidence must prove the workflow stopped or stayed in scope.
controls.buyer_value: Moves SecurityRecipes from governance claims to testable assurance, which is the bar enterprise AI platform teams and acquisition diligence teams will expect.

framework_mappings.control: Measure and Manage
framework_mappings.rationale: Repeatable red-team drills convert agentic failure modes into measured operational controls.

framework_mappings.control: Agent goal hijack, tool misuse, identity abuse, context poisoning, cascading failures, and rogue agents
framework_mappings.rationale: The drill pack directly exercises the highest-risk agentic application failure modes.

framework_mappings.control: Confused deputy prevention, token passthrough avoidance, and session safety
framework_mappings.rationale: MCP-specific drills test whether gateway policy and connector trust controls survive hostile tool context.

framework_mappings.control: AI assurance, monitoring, and control validation
framework_mappings.rationale: The generated pack gives cloud AI programs a portable eval evidence index.
